action_set_definitions.name: get_firewall_state
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves the current firewall configuration including rules, default policy, filtering status, and logging status.

action_set_definitions.name: create_config_snapshot
action_set_definitions.description: Captures a cryptographic snapshot of the current configuration state for backup and audit compliance. Returns a unique snapshot identifier.

label.description: A descriptive label for the snapshot (e.g., 'PRE-INCIDENT-EMERG-INC-5502').

action_set_definitions.name: check_admin_contention
action_set_definitions.description: Checks for existing administrative sessions or configuration locks that might prevent committing changes.

action_set_definitions.name: resolve_contention
action_set_definitions.description: Forcefully clears administrative locks or sessions to allow the current Agent to commit security policies.

lock_id.description: The identifier of the lock to be cleared.

action_set_definitions.name: update_logging_config
action_set_definitions.description: Enables or disables telemetry recording for firewall rules.

enabled.description: Set to true to start recording telemetry/hit counts.

action_set_definitions.name: add_security_rule
action_set_definitions.description: Inserts a new firewall rule into the active policy.

priority.description: The precedence of the rule (lower numbers = higher priority).

action_set_definitions.name: get_hit_count_telemetry
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves the hit count for a specific rule to confirm traffic matches.

rule_id.description: The ID of the rule to check hit counts for.

action_set_definitions.name: restore_snapshot
action_set_definitions.description: Restores the firewall configuration to a previously captured state using a snapshot ID.

snapshot_id.description: The ID of the snapshot to restore.

state_description.rules: A list of firewall rule objects, where each object contains id, priority, direction, protocol, source_ip, destination_ip, port, and action (ALLOW/DENY).
state_description.default_policy: The default action taken if no rules match (e.g., 'DROP' or 'ACCEPT').
state_description.is_active: Indicates whether the firewall engine is currently filtering traffic.
state_description.logging_enabled: Indicates if the firewall is currently recording traffic logs to the internal state.
